_doc: Symbolic conjunction-rule overlay. Each top-level key is a profile_id; entry attaches pathognomonic_combos (AND-conjunctions) and chained_triggers (temporal mechanism chains). Atom labels are matched token-wise against the case PhenotypeFingerprint. See SyndromeCombo / ChainedTrigger pydantic models for the field schema. This file is curated clinical knowledge (HEART/Wells/Centor-style). No code changes are needed to add a new rule; clinical reviewers edit the JSON.

carotid_artery_dissection.label: Carotid or vertebral artery dissection
carotid_artery_dissection.summary: Acute Horner syndrome after neck strain or minor trauma is a vascular emergency until proven otherwise; demands urgent vessel imaging (CTA/MRA) before any massage, manipulation, or topical therapy.

pathognomonic_combos.name: horner_triad_post_neck_strain
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Ipsilateral ptosis + miosis (± anhidrosis) is Horner syndrome; in an adult after neck mechanical strain it indicates arterial wall dissection until vessel imaging excludes it.

infective_endocarditis.label: Infective endocarditis
infective_endocarditis.summary: Recent oral/dental instrumentation followed by acral painful purple lesions and new cardiopulmonary symptoms suggests bacteremic seeding of cardiac valves; demands blood cultures and echocardiography before any dental, dermatology, or symptomatic-only management.

chained_triggers.name: dental_procedure_to_embolic_stigmata
chained_triggers.rationale: Dental procedure → transient bacteremia → seeding of cardiac valves → Osler nodes / Janeway lesions in the periphery + new heart-failure symptoms.

chained_triggers.name: intravenous_drug_use_to_cardiopulm_signs
chained_triggers.rationale: IVDU is a strong endocarditis substrate; new fever plus cardiopulmonary symptoms is endocarditis until proven otherwise.

bacterial_meningitis.label: Bacterial meningitis
bacterial_meningitis.summary: Classical triad — fever, neck stiffness, altered mental status — is a time-critical neurologic emergency; demands CT/LP and empirical broad-spectrum antibiotics before any sedative or psychiatric disposition.

pathognomonic_combos.name: meningitis_classical_triad
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Fever + meningismus + AMS is the textbook triad. Petechial rash raises probability of meningococcal sepsis specifically.

viral_encephalitis.label: Viral (HSV) encephalitis
viral_encephalitis.summary: Fever + altered mental status + behavioural change or hallucinations (with or without neck stiffness) is encephalitis until proven otherwise; demands empirical aciclovir, neuroimaging, and LP — never sedation-only management.

pathognomonic_combos.name: encephalitis_behavioural_triad
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: HSV encephalitis canonically presents with febrile delirium / psychotic-appearing behaviour; meningismus may be absent. Empirical aciclovir is mandatory.

pediatric_bone_malignancy.label: Pediatric bone malignancy (osteosarcoma, Ewing sarcoma, leukemic infiltration)
pediatric_bone_malignancy.summary: In a child or adolescent, bone pain that wakes at night plus constitutional B-symptoms (fevers, weight loss, anorexia) is malignancy until imaging and CBC rule it out, regardless of any antecedent minor trauma.

pathognomonic_combos.name: pediatric_night_bone_pain_with_b_symptoms
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Night-waking bone pain is a textbook red flag for osteosarcoma / Ewing sarcoma; combined with B-symptoms it raises haematologic malignancy. Trauma history does NOT exclude it.

alcoholic_ketoacidosis.label: Alcoholic ketoacidosis
alcoholic_ketoacidosis.summary: Chronic-alcohol patient with vomiting, anorexia, and Kussmaul (deep, rapid) respiration has high anion-gap metabolic acidosis until VBG/electrolytes prove otherwise; demands IV dextrose + thiamine, NOT antiemetic-and-discharge.

pathognomonic_combos.name: kussmaul_with_alcohol_starvation
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Deep rapid breathing in an alcohol-misusing patient who has stopped eating signals AKA / DKA / lactic acidosis — respiratory compensation for metabolic acidosis. Antiemetics alone can mask the underlying acidosis.

pathognomonic_combos.name: tachypnea_with_alcohol_starvation
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Even without explicit Kussmaul labeling, persistent tachypnea after several days of vomiting and alcohol use is the same physiology — metabolic acidosis with respiratory compensation.

acute_pancreatitis.label: Acute pancreatitis
acute_pancreatitis.summary: Persistent severe epigastric pain with vomiting in an alcohol-misusing patient (or one with gallstones) demands lipase, electrolytes, and imaging; antiemetic-only treatment misses necrotizing pancreatitis.

pathognomonic_combos.name: alcohol_pancreatitis_pattern
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Classical alcoholic acute pancreatitis presentation. Tachypnea may also signal AKA — both differentials must be checked.

massive_pulmonary_embolism.label: Massive pulmonary embolism
massive_pulmonary_embolism.summary: Sudden dyspnea + tachycardia after immobility, surgery, or recent cancer treatment demands CTPA / d-dimer; reproducing tachypnea + hypoxia on minimal exertion is a red flag.

chained_triggers.name: immobility_to_acute_dyspnea
chained_triggers.rationale: Long-haul travel, post-op, or cancer-related immobility followed by sudden dyspnea is the classical PE chain.

aortic_dissection.label: Acute aortic dissection
aortic_dissection.summary: Sudden tearing or ripping chest/back pain with hypertension or unequal pulses is dissection until CT angiography excludes it; do NOT discharge as musculoskeletal.

pathognomonic_combos.name: tearing_chest_pain_with_hypertension
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Tearing/ripping pain is the historical pathognomonic descriptor; the combo with hypertension or asymmetric pulses raises pretest probability dramatically.

necrotizing_soft_tissue_infection.label: Necrotizing fasciitis / soft-tissue infection
necrotizing_soft_tissue_infection.summary: Pain out of proportion to skin findings + erythema + fever (± crepitus) is a surgical emergency; antibiotic-only management is a sentinel error.

pathognomonic_combos.name: pain_out_of_proportion_with_erythema_fever
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Pain out of proportion to visible skin change is the textbook early sign before frank skin necrosis appears.

cauda_equina_syndrome.label: Cauda equina syndrome
cauda_equina_syndrome.summary: Saddle anesthesia + new urinary retention or incontinence + low back pain is a spinal-cord emergency requiring urgent MRI and surgical decompression.

pathognomonic_combos.name: saddle_anesthesia_with_retention
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Combination is near-pathognomonic; missing it produces permanent disability.

wernicke_encephalopathy.label: Wernicke encephalopathy
wernicke_encephalopathy.summary: Alcohol misuse + ataxia + ophthalmoplegia or confusion is Wernicke until proven otherwise; demands IV thiamine BEFORE any glucose.

pathognomonic_combos.name: wernicke_classic_triad
pathognomonic_combos.rationale: Even partial triad in an alcohol-misusing patient warrants empiric thiamine; full triad is rare in clinical practice.
